intro: Thrixspermum pygmaeum, commonly known as the miniature thrixspermum, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, wounds, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Thrixspermum pygmaeum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
